key_insight: A mixed finite element discretization of the intrinsic Hodges beam equations can preserve structure while avoiding displacement/rotation variables and algebraic multibody constraints.
engineering_relevance: This enables explicit multibody beam dynamics with cleaner boundary condition handling, better scalability for closed-loop assemblies, and exact energy preservation under suitable time integration.
method: Formulate the beam in intrinsic variables using the Hodges model rather than Reissner-Simo kinematics.
Apply mixed finite elements to the linear differential operators so kinematic and dynamic boundary conditions enter naturally.
Assemble multibody systems through port-Hamiltonian interconnections, eliminating algebraic constraints and keeping nonlinearities in the interconnection operator.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports demonstrations on multiple examples, including closed kinematic loops, and notes exact energy preservation with implicit midpoint time integration plus fewer Newton iterations than prior energy-preserving schemes.
watch_item: The approach relies on careful mixed formulation and implicit midpoint integration, so implementation complexity and solver choice may affect robustness and computational cost.
paper_title: Mixed finite element discretization of intrinsic geometrically exact beams for explicit multibody dynamics
